content: Just as human language learners use written reflection (the reflexion concept from high school English writing workshops) to refine grammatical and narrative choices over repeated practice, large language models can borrow this structured educational framework to improve their self-reflection mechanisms by grounding abstract iterative revision in explicit, language-focused reflective journaling. This turns the generic 'self-reflection' step in LLM agents into a structured writing workshop, where the LLM acts as both student and instructor marking its own output against communicative goals.
